---
title: "Pol 300: Comparative Politics"
subtitle: "Fall 2026 - Franklin Switzerland"
---

![William Conor, State opening of the Northern Ireland Parliament in 1921](https://www.niassembly.gov.uk/globalassets/pagefiles/2968/connor_painting1.jpg)

## Course Information
- Instructor: **Giacomo Lemoli** — [giacomolemoli.github.io](https://giacomolemoli.github.io)
- Instructor’s Email: [glemoli@fus.edu](mailto:glemoli@fus.edu)
- Office location: LAC 8 (3rd floor)
- Office Hours: Monday-Thursday 12:30-14:30
- Class location: TBA
- Class meeting times: Monday-Thursday 14:30-15:45

## Textbook
William R. Clark, Matt Golder, Sona N. Golder, **Foundations of Comparative Politics (2nd Edition)**, 2024, CQ Press

## Recommended Books
The following books are recommended for students who want to cover some of the course modules in more detail.

**Democratization**
- Przeworski, A. (2018). *Why bother with elections?* John Wiley & Sons.
- Ash, T. G. (2010). *The magic lantern: The revolution of'89 witnessed in Warsaw, Budapest, Berlin, and Prague.* Vintage.
- Lieberman, E. (2022). *Until we have won our liberty: South Africa after apartheid.* Princeton University Press
- Tocqueville, A. (1833 [2003]). *Democracy in America: And two essays on America.* Penguin UK.


**Democratic Backsliding**
- Levitsky, S., & Ziblatt, D. (2019). *How democracies die.* Crown.
- Przeworski, A. (2019). *Crises of democracy.* Cambridge University Press.
